audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for women at tomb, rolled stone, young man in white, empty tomb, Galilee message, fear/silence at 16:8, and shorter/longer ending as later textual additions rather than main restored text.

verses.restored: When the Sabbath had passed, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they might go and anoint him.
Very early on the first day of the week, they come to the tomb after the sun had risen.
They were saying to one another, Who will roll away the stone for us from the door of the tomb?
Looking up, they see that the stone has been rolled away, for it was very large.
Entering the tomb, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, clothed in a white robe, and they were alarmed.
He says to them, Do not be alarmed. You seek Jesus the Nazarene, the crucified one. He was raised; he is not here. Look, the place where they laid him.
But go, tell his disciples and Peter that he goes before you into Galilee. There you will see him, just as he told you.
Going out, they fled from the tomb, for trembling and astonishment held them; and they said nothing to anyone, for they were afraid.

textual_notes.note: Many later manuscripts include a longer ending after 16:8. This project keeps 16:8 as the restored primary ending while preserving the longer ending as a textual tradition note rather than erasing it.
Some witnesses include a shorter ending after 16:8. It is noted as a later textual ending, not the main restored reading.
